# Page 1^page=1
Name: Lisa A English | DOB: 7/25/1964 | MRN: 055656 | PCP: Jung Wook Kang Smith, MD | Legal Name: Lisa A English Progress Notes Signed Jun 23, 2026 OCCUPATIONAL THERAPY TREATMENT NOTE Narrative Pt agreeable to OT/PT cotx later in the PM d/t needing time for meds to kick in. Much time as pt anxious and talkative. Questions and concerns discussed and answered at this time. Returned at 1pm as discussed with pt. Pt on bipap t/o tx. Extremely anxious and talkative. Pt sup in bed upon arrival. Reports N, req meds. Rn notified. Pt fixated on N and needing a breathing tx. Reports RT needing to be here at 1pm as well, redirected to tasks t/o tx vcs needed. Tf to EOB independently w bedrail & HOB elevated. Pt asking for furniture to be in places that will not work with personal devices and wiring that she will not allow therapist to move out of the way. Pts anxiousness is a safety concern as pt not thinking clearly/safely and makes a comment about she can't think properly either. Recommendations Recommendation: Pt would benefit from continued therapy at a facility level Equipment Recommended: Rolling walker, Reacher, Sock aid, Long-handled sponge Barriers to discharge: Decreased cognition/safety awareness, Does not have assist needed for safe mobility/balance, Unable to complete basic hygiene This discharge disposition is recommended by OT based on this assessment; however, ultimate discharge disposition will be decided collaboratively with patient, caregivers, and multidisciplinary team. Outcome Measures Daily Activity Raw Score : 15 Daily Activity T-Scale Score: 34.69 Assessment Assessment: Decreased ADL status, Decreased UE ROM, Decreased UE strength, Decreased activity tolerance, Decreased self-care trans, Decreased high-level ADLs, Decreased safe judgement during ADL Plan OT Frequency: 6x/week Treatment Interventions: ADL retraining, Functional transfer training, UE Progress Notes by Logan E at 06/23/26 1328

# Page 2^page=2
MyChart® licensed from Epic Systems Corporation© 1999 - 2026 strengthening/ROM, Activity tolerance, Patient/Family training, Equipment eval/education, Compensatory technique education, Energy conservations/work simplification, Breathing techniques, Safety education, HEP Prior Level of Function Self Care (New): Needed some help Indoor Mobility (Ambulation) (New): Needed some help Stairs (New): Not applicable Functional Cognition (New): Independent Who do you live with?: Adult Child (son) Permanent Residence: Home Home Layout: One level, Ramped entrance ADL Assistance Eating Assistance: Setup or clean-up assistance Oral Hygiene/Grooming Assistance: Supervision or touching assistance, With extra time Shower/Bathe self: Substantial/Maximal assistance UE Dressing Assistance: Partial/moderate assistance, Less than 25% assist needed LE Dressing Assistance: Substantial/Maximal assistance Toilet Hygiene: Dependent Putting on/Taking off footwear: Dependent Functional Transfers Rolling left and right: Supervision or touching assistance Lying to sitting on side of bed: Independent, Using bedrail, HOB elevated Sitting to lying: Up in chair after treatment Sit to Stand: Patient refused Chair/bed to chair transfer: Supervision or touching assistance Balance Sitting - Static: Supervision or touching assistance Sitting - Dynamic: Supervision or touching assistance (SBA/CGA sliding board transfer) Standing - Static: Unable to assess Standing - Dynamic: Unable to assess Logan Engle, OTA
